page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7se.6xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical Intel Xeon 8369B cores running at 2.9 GHz, with a single thread per core configuration. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It utilizes the x86_64 architecture and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 with VNNI support for vector neural network instructions, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and featuring an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.r7se.6xlarge demonstrates solid, mid-tier performance across compute and memory subsystems. Single-core stress-ng performance registers at 1859.59, while multi-core performance reaches 23340.47. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1288.81 GB/sec, which drops to 135.11 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write speed of 65.44 GB/sec. Geekbench scores align with these findings, yielding a single-core score of 1617.0 and a multi-core score of 12181.0. For specialized tasks, the instance achieves a strong text generation speed of 298.41 tokens per second with a small 135M LLM, though performance drops to 15.85 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r7se.6xlarge offers a balanced resource density for memory-heavy workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable CPU performance, avoiding the noisy-neighbor issues of shared-core environments. However, the lack of bundled local storage means users must factor in the latency and cost of external block storage. This instance is highly suitable for memory-intensive applications such as in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise applications, and moderate data processing pipelines.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7se.6xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place its performance in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 12181.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 29197.78. It shows strong efficiency in lightweight LLM text generation at 298.41 tokens per second for a 135M model, and handles Redis SET operations at 23.67 million operations per second. This profile makes the ecs.r7se.6xlarge highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring dedicated compute resources without local storage needs.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7se.6xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, built for in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A dedicated x86 memory-optimized instance featuring high RAM-to-CPU density for demanding in-memory databases and enterprise caching workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs running at 2.9 GHz
192.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration enabled
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and enterprise applications
